title: Cloud-rendering path for ssam sketches in claude.ai/code
globalConstraints: Local macOS workflow must remain behavior-identical (npm install does NOT pull chromium; npm run dev unchanged)
The render-sketch skill's user-facing surface does not change — cloud is an internal branch only
No env-var or host-sniffing detection — presence of .cloud-render/vite.pid is the only cloud-mode signal
Cross-platform cmdline validation (Linux /proc/$pid/cmdline OR macOS ps -o command=) — sketchbook is developed on macOS, runs in Linux sandbox

stories.title: Step 0 — Resolve claude.ai/code provisioning-hook contract
stories.outcome: NO_HOOK_FOUND for any file-based provisioning hook (e.g. .claude/sandbox-setup.sh). claude.ai/code's per-environment Setup Script is UI-configured only. The repo-committed alternative is the SessionStart hook in .claude/settings.json, which runs on every session (incl. resumed), AFTER Claude Code launches, with CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE=true available as the cloud-detection env var. Plan US-005 should be reframed: add a SessionStart hook that lazy-installs chrome-headless-shell when missing, gated by CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE. AC #1's ≤15s cold first render is realistic only when the UI-configured Setup Script has run OR a prior session's filesystem snapshot persists the browser. Otherwise the first render of a fresh environment pays the ~30-45s install (covered by AC #8).
acceptanceCriteria: Anthropic/claude.ai/code documentation is consulted via web search for the actual project-level setup-hook convention
Outcome recorded: either a real documented hook path, or an explicit 'no hook supported' decision
If no hook is supported, AC #1 (cold first render ≤15s) is annotated as conditional in the plan and the lazy-install path becomes the only entry point
No invented hook filename — only a documented path or no path

stories.title: Step 1 — Add cloud-only deps + scripts to package.json
acceptanceCriteria: package.json has playwright-core in devDependencies
package.json has tsx in devDependencies (if not already present)
package.json has proper-lockfile in devDependencies (used by cloud-render for render.lock)
package.json scripts include cloud:install, cloud:render, cloud:stop, cloud:smoke
A fresh npm install does NOT trigger a Playwright browser download (verified by absence of a Playwright postinstall script and the install being gated behind cloud:install)
Local user running `npm run dev` after the change still gets the same experience

stories.title: Step 4 — Set window.__ssam_ready from first-frame callback in src/index.ts
acceptanceCriteria: src/index.ts sets (window as any).__ssam_ready = true from inside the sketch's first wrap.render invocation, NOT after the dynamic import resolves
The assignment is gated behind import.meta.env.DEV so production builds are unaffected
The flag is reset to undefined before each new sketch module loads so HMR re-mounts cannot leave a stale true value
Existing local Mac dev workflow is unaffected — npm run dev with a 2D sketch still runs, displays, and supports HMR

stories.title: Step 3 — Implement scripts/cloud-render.ts per normative pseudocode
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at scripts/cloud-render.ts and is invocable via `npm run cloud:render -- <sketchPath>`
On start: mkdir -p output && mkdir -p .cloud-render, then acquires exclusive lock on .cloud-render/render.lock via proper-lockfile
Registers SIGINT and SIGTERM handlers that release the lock and kill any spawned Chromium child before exiting
Validates .cloud-render/vite.pid by checking kill -0 AND that the process cmdline contains 'vite' — cross-platform: /proc/$pid/cmdline on Linux, `ps -o command= -p $pid` on macOS
On Vite respawn: SIGTERM the old process, poll port 5173 (via lsof or net check) until free OR 5s elapsed, then SIGKILL if still alive
Writes { pid, sketch, port } JSON to .cloud-render/vite.pid
Redirects spawned Vite stdout/stderr to .cloud-render/vite.log
On chrome-headless-shell missing: runs `npm run cloud:install`, captures exit code, on failure writes .cloud-render/install.log and exits non-zero with an actionable error
Launches Chromium with --use-gl=swiftshader --enable-unsafe-swiftshader --ignore-gpu-blocklist --no-sandbox --disable-dev-shm-usage
Waits for window.__ssam_ready === true with a 15s timeout
Issues GET /export from Node (using global fetch or undici), NOT via page.evaluate inside Chromium — 12s timeout
Closes Chromium and releases the lock before exiting
Prints the JSON filename from /export response to stdout (so the skill can parse it)
Subcommand `cloud:render stop` (or `npm run cloud:stop`): SIGTERM the Vite PID, port-free poll, SIGKILL if needed, remove vite.pid

stories.title: Step 2 — SessionStart hook + .gitignore (reframed per US-001 findings)
acceptanceCriteria: .claude/settings.json includes a SessionStart hook that runs scripts/cloud-bootstrap.sh on startup and resume

scripts/cloud-bootstrap.sh: exits 0 silently when CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE != 'true' (local Mac path); when in cloud, checks for chromium-headless-shell binary under .cloud-render/browsers and runs `npm run cloud:install` only if missing (idempotent + fast on cached sessions)
.gitignore includes .cloud-render/
docs/cloud-rendering.md documents the recommended UI-configured Setup Script content (faster path) AND the SessionStart hook as the auto-fallback

stories.title: Step 5 — Update render-sketch skill with cloud branch
acceptanceCriteria: .claude/skills/render-sketch/SKILL.md is updated such that presence of .cloud-render/vite.pid is the canonical cloud-mode signal
If vite.pid is absent → local Mac browser flow (today's behavior, unchanged)
If vite.pid is present → skill invokes `npm run cloud:render -- <sketchPath>` and parses the printed filename
No env-var or host detection logic in the skill
Step numbering / Troubleshooting section reflects the new branch

stories.title: Step 6 — Smoke test + WebGL test sketch + 2D test sketch
acceptanceCriteria: src/sketches/_test/cloud-render-2d.ts: a deterministic 2D ssam sketch (mode '2d'), simple colored composition, exposes mcp:export handler
src/sketches/_test/cloud-render-webgl.ts: a minimal ssam sketch with mode 'webgl' or 'webgl2', renders a fullscreen non-uniform shader gradient, exposes mcp:export handler
scripts/cloud-render-smoke.ts: drives cloud-render against both test sketches, asserts each PNG exists, file size > 1KB, and pixel variance > 0 along a sampled row near image middle
Smoke records wall-clock per render and prints a labeled table at exit
Smoke exits 0 only if assertions pass AND timings are within AC budgets (first render ≤15s, warm ≤7s, switch ≤15s) with 20% sandbox-jitter slack
Smoke cleans up after itself: kills Vite, removes any locked state, leaves .cloud-render/ in a clean state

stories.title: Step 7 — Author docs/cloud-rendering.md
acceptanceCriteria: docs/cloud-rendering.md exists, ~60 lines
States the provisioning model resolved in US-001 (hook path or 'no hook — lazy install')
Describes manual session bootstrap commands: npm install && npm run cloud:install
Documents debugging: tail .cloud-render/vite.log, cat .cloud-render/install.log
Includes a top-of-file note: 'Local users: ignore this file — it's only relevant in claude.ai/code'
Documents the --no-sandbox security tradeoff (localhost-only navigation)

stories.title: Verification — local regression + typecheck + deferred cloud smoke
stories.outcome: Verified: (1) npx tsc --noEmit on scripts/cloud-render.ts + scripts/cloud-render-smoke.ts reports zero errors. (2) Vite dev server starts on port 5173 with the modified src/index.ts and serves /src/index.ts with HTTP 200 — local Mac flow behavior-identical. (3) npm install completed exit 0 without writing playwright-core/.local-browsers (no project-tree chromium download). NOTE: `npm run build` fails with a top-level-await error, but this is PRE-EXISTING (confirmed by stashing src/index.ts and re-running — fails identically). Unrelated to this work; can be fixed by raising vite build.target to es2022 but that's out of scope. DEFERRED: end-to-end `npm run cloud:smoke` not run on macOS because it would download ~60MB of chromium-headless-shell to .cloud-render/browsers — pollution the user explicitly excluded via AC #6. The full smoke must run in the user's claude.ai/code session.
acceptanceCriteria: TypeScript typecheck (tsc --noEmit) on new scripts exits 0
Existing local Mac workflow is unchanged: `npm run dev` starts and serves on 5173
`npm install` from a clean state does NOT download chromium binaries to the project tree
End-to-end smoke deferral documented if cloud:smoke would pollute the local dev environment
